topic: Whether to pursue a broader VP role at a smaller medtech company
situation: The role offers broader authority but less scale and an earlier-stage organization.
decision: Decide whether the mandate compounds long-term product-executive growth.
stakeholders: Candidate, family, prospective CEO, product and engineering teams
constraints: Need credible authority, resources, and a clinically meaningful product.
evidence: Track record in startups and large medtech companies.
uncertainty: Actual decision rights, financing runway, executive alignment, and team quality.
desired_outcome: A decision framework and diligence plan.
initial_recommendation: Pursue the conversation because broader authority may accelerate the desired trajectory.
useful: Focuses on mandate rather than title.
weak: Underweights financing risk and the cost of rebuilding basic operating systems.
unsupported_assumptions: Assumes the CEO will delegate meaningful product authority.
missing_context: Board expectations, runway, clinical evidence, and existing team capability.
risks: Title inflation, insufficient resources, and unclear market readiness.
original_question: Should I pursue this VP role?
refined_question: Under what conditions would this VP mandate compound my trajectory enough to justify stage and execution risk?
refined_response: Proceed only to structured diligence. Require evidence of decision authority, runway, product quality, team capacity, and executive alignment before treating it as a real opportunity.
human_judgment: The title alone is not valuable. The opportunity is attractive only if the CEO wants an actual product executive and the company has enough evidence and runway to support the mandate.
final_decision: Take the first conversation and use a written diligence scorecard before investing further.
rationale: The conversation has option value, while the scorecard prevents title-driven enthusiasm.
alternatives: Decline immediately; pursue only larger companies.
dissent: The search could distract from development in the current role.
next_action: Prepare five mandate and runway questions.
prompt_lesson: Ask for conditions and disconfirming evidence instead of a binary recommendation.
